summary: Improved matching in src/melee/lb/lbshadow.c for lbShadow_8000E9F0 and lbShadow_8000F38C. The PR adjusted spline parameter handling and tangent math, replaced a sqrtf call with a file-local __frsqrte-based helper, and made several variable lifetime/order changes to improve shadow update codegen. Automated decomp.dev reported lbShadow_8000E9F0 improving from 93.27% to 95.81% and lbShadow_8000F38C from 99.15% to 99.70%.

key_files.reason: Only changed file; contains matching improvements for lbShadow_8000E9F0 spline tangent code and lbShadow_8000F38C shadow update code.

what_changed: In lbShadow_8000E9F0, preserved the original spline parameter as orig_u before scaling u by spline->numcv - 1, then used orig_u for the exact endpoint check against 1.0F.
In lbShadow_8000E9F0, changed the scaled-parameter flow from t = u * (...) to mutating u, deriving idx from u, and computing t as the fractional remainder.
In the spline type 1 tangent math, reordered and algebraically rewrote bez1 from -((4.0F * t) - 1.0F) to 1.0F - (4.0F * t), with t2 computed first.
Added static inline f32 lbShadow_Sqrtf(f32 x), using __frsqrte on a f64 cast, three Newton-style refinement steps with f64 half/three constants, a volatile f32 y, and a local u8 padding array.
Replaced the guarded sqrtf(dist) block in lbShadow_8000F38C with dist = lbShadow_Sqrtf(dist).
Adjusted lbShadow_8000F38C local declarations by hoisting nextGx, hasObj, i, and shadow2 to the function-scope local block.
Reduced PAD_STACK in lbShadow_8000F38C from 0x48 to 0x10.

Changed several code-shaping details in lbShadow_8000F38C: reusing nextGx instead of declaring it inside the GX link loop, assigning upVec.x before upVec.y, splitting the HSD_JObj cast into a jobj local before HSD_ShadowAddObject, and reusing hoisted shadow2 and i locals.
smart_moves: Kept orig_u separate from scaled u so the endpoint comparison still tests the original normalized spline parameter while allowing the scaled value to drive idx and t, a useful way to match both logic and instruction flow.
Used algebraic equivalence and statement reordering in the bezier tangent path to influence generated code without changing the apparent math.

Matched the target sqrt behavior with an inline __frsqrte refinement sequence instead of a library sqrtf call, including volatile/local-stack details that can affect instruction selection and stack layout.
Hoisted locals out of inner blocks to steer register allocation and stack layout in a nearly matched function.

Made small ordering changes, such as upVec assignment order and introducing a jobj temporary, which are typical late-stage decomp tactics when semantics are already understood but codegen still differs.
Documented verification with python configure.py && ninja and tools/checkdiff.py for both touched functions.
decomp_lessons: For spline code, preserve original normalized parameters when endpoint behavior depends on pre-scaled u; matching may require both original and scaled forms to exist as distinct locals.
Late-stage matching can hinge on algebraically equivalent expressions and assignment order, not only on larger control-flow structure.
Do not assume sqrtf calls match the original binary; PowerPC-era code may require explicit __frsqrte plus refinement and float-store behavior to reproduce codegen.
Variable scope is a matching lever: hoisting a temporary from an inner block to the parent function can alter register allocation enough to improve similarity.
PAD_STACK values may need to be revisited after adding/removing locals or changing inline math helpers; this PR reduced lbShadow_8000F38C padding from 0x48 to 0x10.
Use decomp.dev/checkdiff byte and percentage deltas to confirm that small code-shaping edits actually improve the target functions.
naming_conventions: Existing unmatched functions keep the project address-style subsystem naming: lbShadow_8000E9F0 and lbShadow_8000F38C.
The new helper uses the subsystem prefix and math role, lbShadow_Sqrtf, and is static inline because it is a file-local matching helper rather than a known external symbol.
orig_u clearly names the unscaled spline parameter while u is reused for the scaled segment parameter.

Temporary names in the diff follow local purpose or existing project style: nextGx for next_gx traversal, hasObj for shadow object state, shadow2 for an additional HSD_Shadow pointer, and jobj for an HSD_JObj cast.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Mutate u in place after saving orig_u to change temporary lifetime and likely register use around idx/t calculation.
Rewrite -((4.0F * t) - 1.0F) as 1.0F - (4.0F * t) and move t2 calculation earlier to affect floating-point instruction ordering.
Inline a custom sqrt approximation using __frsqrte, f64 constants, repeated refinement, and a volatile f32 store to avoid the prior sqrtf call shape.
Use a dummy local u8 _[0x38] = { 0 } inside lbShadow_Sqrtf, apparently as a stack/codegen shaper.
Hoist nextGx, hasObj, i, and shadow2 declarations to the outer local list to influence register allocation and stack layout.
Introduce HSD_JObj* jobj before HSD_ShadowAddObject rather than casting directly at the call site.
Reduce PAD_STACK in lbShadow_8000F38C after local and helper changes.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
The automated decomp.dev report for GALE01 listed two unmatched-item improvements: lbShadow_8000E9F0 gained +22 bytes, 93.27% to 95.81%; lbShadow_8000F38C gained +9 bytes, 99.15% to 99.70%.
follow_up_queries: Search for other unmatched sqrtf calls that may need __frsqrte-based inline helpers.
Continue lbShadow_8000E9F0 matching around HSD_Spline endpoint and tangent calculations.
Review remaining lbShadow_8000F38C mismatches for variable scope, PAD_STACK, and temporary-cast codegen effects.
Compare nearby lbshadow functions for repeated shadow update patterns involving HSD_ShadowAddObject, HSD_ShadowDeleteObject, and HSD_ViewingRectInit.
